RootWebArea, Taylor Swift albums discography - Wikipedia
  main
    sectionheader
      navigation, Contents
        [Toggle the table of contents](click:5269)
      heading, Taylor Swift albums discography
      [Go to an article in another language. Available in 7 languages](click:5278)
    paragraph
      The American singer-songwriter [Taylor Swift](click:5478) has released 12 original albums, 4 [re-recorded albums](click:5479), 5 [extended plays (EPs)](click:5480), and 4 [live albums](click:5481). In the United States, as of May 2025, she had sold 116.7 million [album-equivalent units](click:5484), coming from 54 million pure sales and 70.7 billion [streams](click:5485);[[1]](click:5487) the [Recording Industry Association of America](click:5490) (RIAA) as of November 2025[certified](click:5493) 110 million album units, making her the [highest-certified female album artist](click:5494).[[2]](click:5496) On the [Billboard 200](click:5499) chart, she has accumulated 15 albums and 96 total weeks at number one, more than any other solo act.[[3]](click:5502)
    paragraph
      Swift released six albums with [Big Machine Records](click:5506). Her debut album, [Taylor Swift](click:5507) (2006), spent more weeks on the Billboard 200 than any other album in the 2000s decade,[[4]](click:5511) and her second, [Fearless](click:5515) (2008), was the only album from the 2000s decade to spend one year in the top 10 of the Billboard 200.[[5]](click:5518) Fearless was Swift's first album to top the charts in Australia, Canada, and New Zealand.[[6]](click:5523) Her next albums—[Speak Now](click:5527) (2010), [Red](click:5529) (2012), [1989](click:5531) (2014), and [Reputation](click:5532) (2017)—all reached number one on the Billboard 200, and each of them sold over one million US first-week copies.[[7]](click:5536) All four albums reached number one in Australia, Canada, and New Zealand;[[8]](click:5540)[[9]](click:5544) and the latter three all reached number one in the United Kingdom.[[10]](click:5548)
    paragraph
      Under a recording contract with [Republic Records](click:5552) signed in 2018,[[11]](click:5554) Swift has released six original albums—[Lover](click:5558) (2019), [Folklore](click:5559) (2020), [Evermore](click:5562) (2020), [Midnights](click:5564) (2022), [The Tortured Poets Department](click:5566) (2024), and [The Life of a Showgirl](click:5568) (2025)—all of them peaked atop the Billboard 200.[[12]](click:5571) Lover was Swift's first to reach number one in Spain and Sweden.[[13]](click:5576)[[14]](click:5580) Midnights, The Tortured Poets Department, and The Life of a Showgirl each sold one million first-week copies in the United States,[[12]](click:5587)[[15]](click:5591) and they reached number one in Australia, Canada,[[8]](click:5595) Denmark,[[16]](click:5599) Ireland,[[17]](click:5603) New Zealand,[[9]](click:5607) Spain,[[13]](click:5611) Norway,[[18]](click:5615) and the United Kingdom.[[9]](click:5619) In the United States, The Tortured Poets Department was Swift's first album to open with two million units first-week,[[15]](click:5624) and The Life of a Showgirl opened with four million units, becoming the [fastest-selling album in American history](click:5628).[[12]](click:5630)
    paragraph
      Swift's departure from Big Machine resulted in a [dispute over the ownership of her first six albums](click:5634) in 2019, which resulted in her re-recording four albums—[Fearless (Taylor's Version)](click:5636) (2021), [Red (Taylor's Version)](click:5638) (2021), [Speak Now (Taylor's Version)](click:5640) (2023), and [1989 (Taylor's Version)](click:5642) (2023)—all of which reached number one in Australia, Canada,[[8]](click:5644) Ireland,[[17]](click:5648) New Zealand,[[9]](click:5652) the United Kingdom,[[10]](click:5656) and the United States.[[19]](click:5660) Fearless (Taylor's Version) was the first re-recorded album to top the Billboard 200,[[20]](click:5666) and 1989 (Taylor's Version) sold over a million US first-week copies.[[21]](click:5671) Swift holds the record for the [most annual best-selling albums in the United States](click:5674)—eight times: Fearless in 2009, 1989 in 2014, Reputation in 2017, Lover in 2019, Folklore in 2020, Midnights in 2022, 1989 (Taylor's Version) in 2023, and The Tortured Poets Department in 2024.[[22]](click:5684)
